entries.reason: applyReconcileResultsCapability is a stub: reconcile_results entries are not replayed through a non-persisted TestRun, so stale (incl. persistence-restored) exclusive-group icons are never overwritten (keel/issue-89 reopened; the CR-121 removal mechanism was falsified by owner live validation).
Current discovery still emits the pre-approved a/C/d tree and has no B - Desired State group.
Current VSIX publishDiscovery replaces the TestController collection and creates new TestItem instances.
Current run-event application allows an explicit test_started event after the same item reached a terminal state.
Current real binary has no dry-run resolution mode for sweeping every discovery-served runnable id.

applyReconcileNoResultCapability is a stub: nothing drops a non-active exclusive member's rendered result on an at-rest discovery refresh; only an activation-run cleared event does (keel/issue-89, rca-3 root #1).

On a real vscode.TestController the exclusive-group deactivated sibling keeps its result because invalidateClearedResults only calls invalidateTestResults (marks the result outdated, not removed); cr-115 rebuilds/removes the sibling item to drop it.
